description_long: This restaurant features a rectangular, multi-level structure with a stucco-like beige exterior and a series of dark brown, tiled roof sections. The front facade is adorned with a large arched entrance framed by decorative elements and flanked by small, square windows. Two prominent, boxy side extensions with flat, grid-patterned roofs project from the main building, likely serving as additional rooms or dining areas. The roof is segmented into four large, grid-like skylights or solar panels, suggesting a modern touch. The rear and side walls are mostly plain, with minimal ornamentation and a few small windows or doors. The overall appearance combines traditional adobe or southwestern influences with contemporary geometric forms.
description: A beige, stucco-walled restaurant with a large arched entrance, dark tiled roofs, and boxy side extensions. The design blends traditional southwestern elements with modern geometric features.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main arched entrance with decorative trim and small windows, as well as the textured, tiled roof and one of the side extensions.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the two boxy side extensions with grid-patterned roofs and a plain, unadorned back wall.
description_view_2: The side view displays the length of the building, the textured roof, a large vertical door or window, and the side extension with a small window.
description_view_3: The top-down view emphasizes the four large, grid-like roof sections and the stepped, rectangular form of the building.
attribute_facade_design: The facade features a textured, beige stucco finish with a prominent arched entrance framed by decorative trim. Dark brown, tiled roof sections add contrast, while small windows and minimal ornamentation create a clean, geometric look. The overall style is both traditional and modern, with boxy side extensions and grid-patterned roof elements.
